explanation: This command adds an explicit /AutoIt3ExecuteScript flag to the original command, which is a known trigger in the Sigma rule. It simulates a renamed AutoIt3.exe execution and is a valid test for the detection rule.
This command substitutes the / flag with a - flag, which is a valid syntax variation in some Windows command-line tools. It still matches the Sigma rule's detection logic.
This command adds the /ErrorStdOut flag, which is another known trigger in the Sigma rule. It simulates a renamed AutoIt3.exe execution with additional flags.
This command includes both /AutoIt3ExecuteScript and /ErrorStdOut flags, which are both triggers in the Sigma rule. It provides a more comprehensive test of the detection logic.
This command uses - instead of / for both flags, which is a valid syntax substitution in some Windows command-line tools. It still matches the Sigma rule's detection logic.

This command adds the /AutoIt3ExecuteScript flag to the original command, which is a known trigger in the Sigma rule. It simulates a renamed AutoIt3.exe execution and is a valid test for the detection rule.
